tldr: Superwall handles in-app subscription paywalls, while OpenX is a programmatic SSP and ad exchange. Moving means rebuilding monetization around real-time bidding and header bidding rather than paywall conversion.
why_switch: Add CTV and web display alongside mobile
what_to_export: A/B test history and winning variants
export_method: Use the Superwall dashboard's standard export options to pull paywall definitions, campaign configurations, and analytics reports. Capture A/B test results and segment definitions through the reporting interface. From your codebase, archive trigger names, placement identifiers, and audience filters since paywalls are referenced this way at runtime. Save image and copy assets locally. If anything is not exposed through the UI, contact support for a one-off export instead of relying on unofficial endpoints. Keep webhook payload schemas for any analytics or CRM integrations that need rewiring.
import_method: OpenX onboarding usually goes through their publisher sales team, which provisions an account and inventory. Integrate OpenX through their SDK or via a header bidding wrapper such as Prebid; for in-app, the standard route is through a mediation platform that supports OpenX as a bidder. Define ad units corresponding to where paywalls used to appear, configure bid floors, and connect reporting. Validate fill, latency, and revenue in a staged rollout. Because OpenX is more of a demand source than a turnkey monetization product, expect to pair it with a mediation layer and not call it directly the way you called Superwall.
gotchas: OpenX is a demand source and typically requires a mediation layer for in-app, not a one-to-one Superwall replacement
Header bidding setup adds operational complexity that small teams may underestimate
Pricing is custom and revenue share is opaque; model assumed eCPM conservatively
App-specific feature investment is lighter than mobile-first platforms
Switching revenue from subscriptions to programmatic ads changes LTV calculations significantly
Latency in bid responses can affect UX where paywalls used to render instantly

email.subject: Migration update: moving from Superwall to OpenX over the next 14 days
email.body:
Hi there,

We're rolling out a change to our monetization stack over the next two weeks, moving from Superwall paywalls to OpenX programmatic demand. The goal is to diversify revenue across mobile, web, and CTV inventory.

A few things to expect:
- Existing subscriptions remain active; nothing changes for current subscribers.
- New users may see programmatic ads where paywalls previously appeared.
- We'll watch performance closely and tune ad density to keep the experience clean.

If anything looks off during the rollout, reply to this email and we'll dig in.

Thanks for sticking with us.

Your customer success team
